changelogCursor.title: get_scf_submissions now returns the full application/proposal text

apiKey.description: LumenLoop API key (starts with `llmcp_`). Tiers: guest < partner < read < write < manager < admin. Contact LumenLoop for a key.

oauth.description: OAuth access token from the LumenLoop authorization server. Discovery: /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource. New OAuth identities default to the guest tier.

info.description: REST access to the LumenLoop Stellar-ecosystem content platform. Every operation is a tool call: POST /tools/{name} with JSON arguments (base URL https://api.lumenloop.com/v1). The same tools are available over MCP at mcp.lumenloop.com. Authenticate with `Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ API key or OAuth access token>`. Start at the base URL (index), /tools (catalog), /docs (guide).
info.title: LumenLoop API

post.description: Paid via the x402 protocol: the first call returns HTTP 402 with payment requirements in the `payment-required` response header (base64 JSON; exact scheme, USDC on Stellar). Pay with an x402-capable client and retry with the `X-PAYMENT` header; on settlement the amount is credited to your billing account and the settlement transaction is returned in the `X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE` header. Requires a billing account on your credential (409 otherwise). 503 when payments are not enabled on this deployment.

parameters.description: USD amount to credit (1–500; default set by the server).
parameters.name: amount

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

200.description: Payment settled; credit recorded.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.
Actionable next step for the caller.
Actionable next step for the caller.

402.description: Payment required — x402 terms in the payment-required header.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

409.description: Credential has no billing account to credit.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

503.description: x402 payments not configured on this deployment.

post.summary: Top up allocated research credits — pay USDC on Stellar (x402)

get.description: Public, no auth. Every deploy-worthy API/MCP change, newest first. Filter with ?since=YYYY-MM-DD (entries dated on or after that day; 400 on malformed dates) and ?breaking=true (only changes an integration could parse-depend on).

parameters.description: YYYY-MM-DD — only entries dated on or after this day (inclusive).
parameters.name: since

200.description: Changelog entries, newest first.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

get.description: Returns the principal, granted tiers, applicable rate limits, tool counts, and — for partner billing accounts — monthly quota, month-to-date spend, and allocated credits (total + remaining).

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

post.description: The relationship path — a human reviews and applies the change. Distinct from POST /billing/topup, which credits your account instantly via x402. At most one open request per account (409 while one is pending). kind="quota" asks to raise the recurring monthly allowance; kind="credits" asks for a one-time allocation.

post.description:
Find specific passages inside long AV transcripts via per-chunk embedding similarity. Use this when you want to quote what was said, not just "which video". Returns the parent video metadata plus the matched chunk text and its start offset.

**When to use:** Find WHERE in videos/podcasts/spaces a topic is discussed — returns AI summaries of the matching passages plus an opaque ordering offset (NOT a playback timestamp). Use when you need "the part of the talk where they discuss X".

**Returns:** Array of AV items with AI summary, created_at (the recording's date — use it to judge and cite how recent the talk is), start_offset (an opaque transcript chunk offset for ordering passages within a recording — NOT playback seconds; do not render it as a timestamp or build a deep-link from it), and link. Transcript text itself is never returned — cite the link + the passage summary.

query.description: Topic or phrase to match against transcript passages

results.description: Array of AV items: AI summary, start_offset (opaque transcript chunk offset, not seconds), link.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

post.description:
Find articles, AV content, events, and tweets that are semantically related to a project. Uses embedding similarity to find cross-domain content that DISCUSSES the project. Supports optional date/source/type filters for scoped research.

**When to use:** All published content related to ONE project, keyed BY SLUG (resolve a name to a slug with search_directory first). For an open-ended topic use search_content_semantic; for entity-name mentions (incl. tokens/orgs) where you only have a name use find_content_by_entity.

**Returns:** Content grouped by type with AI summaries + source links + date, filtered to the project, recency-aware so recent coverage (incidents, launches) surfaces alongside canonical content. response_format 'concise' (external default) omits long_summary; pass 'detailed' to include it.

date_field.description: For articles only: force a specific date field. Default is COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at).

date_start.description: ISO date/timestamp lower bound (inclusive). For articles, applies to COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at) by default to avoid stale re-scrape pollution.

limit.description: Max results per content type (default 20). Effective rows = limit × number of types — keep it small and narrow `types` instead of raising it.

response_format.description: 'concise' omits long_summary from each row (smaller payloads); 'detailed' keeps it. Default: detailed.

sources.description: Filter by source. For articles: domain. For AV: channel. For events: platform. For tweets: user_screen_name.

types.description: Restrict to specific content types. Default: all 9 (knowledge = internal dated notes). Narrow this for smaller, faster results — fanning out across all types multiplies the row count.

articles.description: Related articles with AI summaries + source links.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

post.description:
Find articles, AV, events, governance proposals, and SCF submissions that mention a specific named entity. Uses LLM-extracted entities (project/person/organization/token) from content_entities. Case-insensitive match on entity_name or exact match on normalized_slug. Use this when you want exact entity grounding, not semantic similarity.

**When to use:** Find content mentioning a NAMED entity (project, organization, token) by case-insensitive name match with LLM-confidence filtering — works even when the entity has no directory slug. If you have a slug, prefer find_content_about_project.

**Returns:** Array of content items with the matched entity, confidence score, and content metadata.

content_type.description: Restrict to a specific content type

entity.description: Entity name (case-insensitive) or normalized slug. Examples: "Aquarius", "aquarius", "StellarOrg".

entity_type.description: Restrict to a specific entity type

min_confidence.description: Minimum LLM confidence to include (0.0-1.0). Default 0.5 filters out weak matches.

results.description: Array of content items with matched entity, confidence, metadata.

Find projects semantically similar to a given project using embedding cosine similarity. More accurate than tag/category matching.

**When to use:** Find projects similar to a known project (by slug) via embedding similarity — better than tag/category matching. For keyword search use search_directory.

**Returns:** Array of projects ranked by cosine similarity: slug, title, description, similarity score.

post.description:
Search SCF (Stellar Community Fund) submissions by embedding similarity. Pass a natural-language query for topic/category discovery or an existing submission slug to find similar prior proposals. Returns title, description, category, round, award_type, the linked_project_slug (the primary directory project, if any), and linked_project_slugs (the full set of directory projects this submission credits).

**When to use:** Explore SCF submissions by topic (natural-language query) or find proposals similar to an existing one (slug). Query and slug are mutually exclusive.

**Returns:** Array of submissions ranked by similarity, with round/category/funding metadata, linked_project_slug (the primary credited directory project) and linked_project_slugs (the full set of credited directory projects).

category.description: Restrict to a specific category (e.g. "Developer Tooling")

query.description: Natural-language query (e.g. "ZK proofs for compliance"). Mutually exclusive with `slug`.

round.description: Restrict to a specific SCF round (e.g. "SCF #29")

slug.description: Existing SCF submission slug (e.g. "recsAQHCghw9HIUvF") — returns semantically similar submissions. Mutually exclusive with `query`.

results.description: Array of similar submissions with round/category/funding, linked_project_slug (primary credited project) and linked_project_slugs (full credited set).

post.description:
Get details of a project from the ecosystem directory by slug. Pass compact=true to get a slim writer-friendly shape (~500 chars: slug, title, short description, category, tags, twitter_handles, website, other_names) — recommended for writer agents that just need to verify a slug and read @ handles. Default returns the full row (~8k chars including conflict tracking, source values, field policy).

**When to use:** Fetch one project BY SLUG. If you only have a name, resolve it to a slug with search_directory first — a guessed slug will 404. Pass compact=true when you only need identity fields for writing/citation (cuts ~95% of the payload).

**Returns:** One project object: slug, title, description, category, tags, links (website, x, github, …), based_in, operating_region, SCF awards. Internal-only fields (source values, conflicts, hidden links) are never included for external callers.

compact.description: Return slim writer shape instead of full row. Cuts ~95% of payload size.

post.description:
Browse/enumerate ONE collection by structured criteria — status, source, date, time period (upcoming/past events) — with sorting and pagination. Its optional "search" is the SAME exact case-insensitive SUBSTRING match on title and URL as search_documents (not body text, not meaning). For conceptual or question-phrased discovery, or when you do not know which collection holds the answer, use search_content_semantic instead. The av collection accepts either "videos" or "av".

**When to use:** Browse/page through a content collection (articles, events, videos/av, research) with filters and sorting. For text matching use search_documents; for meaning-based discovery use search_content_semantic.

**Returns:** Page of document summaries plus pagination info. External callers get summary projection: title, url, slug, summary, long_summary, tags, dates — never full article bodies or AV transcripts.

collection.description: Content collection to query (videos and av both target the av table)

period.description: Events only: filter by time period

sort.description: Sort field. Articles: processed_at, publishing_date, created_at, domain, source. Events: start_at, processed_at, created_at. Videos: processed_at, publishing_date, created_at, source. Jobs: updated_at, last_seen_at, published_at, created_at.

items.description: Document summaries: title, url, slug, summary, long_summary, tags, dates.

post.summary: List research drafts with optional filters

post.description:
Search all content semantically. Embeds the query text and finds similar articles, AV, events, and tweets via pgvector cosine distance. Use this (not search_documents/list_documents) whenever the query is conceptual or phrased as a question, or when you do not know which collection holds the answer; reserve search_documents for exact title/URL keyword lookup. Supports date/source/type filters — use these for scoped research queries like "find Stellar DeFi content from March" or "what did SDF post about privacy".

**When to use:** Meaning-based search across ALL published content types at once (articles, AV, events, research) — the default discovery tool for "what has been said about X". For exact-term lookup in one collection use search_documents; for content about one specific project use find_content_about_project. For "latest/current" ecosystem facts (protocol version, program/fund status, recent incidents or launches) prefer this over live web — results are dated and the corpus updates continuously; cite the date. Use web only for facts the corpus cannot hold, e.g. upstream package/registry pins (crates.io, npm, GitHub release tags).

**Returns:** Results grouped by content type, each with AI summary, source url, date, and similarity score — never full text. Cite the returned url. response_format 'concise' (external default) omits long_summary; pass 'detailed' to include it.

date_field.description: For articles only: force a specific date field. Default is COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at).

date_start.description: ISO date/timestamp lower bound (inclusive). For articles, applies to COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at) by default to avoid stale re-scrape pollution.

limit.description: Max results per content type (default 20). Effective rows = limit × number of types — keep it small and narrow `types` instead of raising it.

response_format.description: 'concise' omits long_summary from each row (smaller payloads); 'detailed' keeps it. Default: detailed.

sources.description: Filter by source. For articles: domain. For AV: channel. For events: platform. For tweets: user_screen_name.

types.description: Restrict to specific content types. Default: all 9 (knowledge = internal dated notes). Narrow this for smaller, faster results — fanning out across all types multiplies the row count.

post.summary: Search all content semantically

post.description:
Search the Stellar ecosystem directory for projects by name, description, or category (substring match). If a multi-word or conceptual query matches no substring, it automatically falls back to semantic project discovery (response carries match_mode: "semantic") so you still get the closest projects.

**When to use:** Start here to find a Stellar ecosystem project by name, keyword, or category when you do not know its slug. Substring match, but a multi-word/conceptual query that hits no substring auto-falls back to semantic project discovery, so you can also use it for "what RWA/wallet/oracle projects exist" style asks. For "projects like X" use find_similar_projects_semantic; for content ABOUT a project use find_content_about_project.

**Returns:** { count, projects: [...] } — each project: slug, title, description, category, tags. When the substring match is empty and the semantic fallback fires, the response also carries match_mode: "semantic" + a note, and rows include a similarity score. Take the slug into get_project for full details.

For conceptual or question-phrased queries use search_content_semantic; to browse a collection by status/date/source use list_documents. The av collection accepts either "videos" or "av".

**When to use:** Exact/keyword match on title or URL within one collection (e.g. find the article about a known announcement). For conceptual queries ("content about cross-border payments") use search_content_semantic.

quirk: GET /v1/tools and GET /v1/skills hide (or mark unavailable) partner-tier items even with a partner key. This inventory unions the list endpoints and validates the tool count against GET /v1/me tools.available. Partner-tier items are persisted as name-only stubs (partner_stub: true) — detail is never committed.

skills.description: Connect your AI assistant (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Cursor, or any MCP client) to LumenLoop's free read-only Stellar ecosystem MCP at https://mcp.lumenloop.com and learn its 18 query tools. Use when the user wants to set up or troubleshoot the lumenloop connector, asks what Stellar ecosystem data is available, or needs to know which lumenloop skill fits a task.
skills.name: lumenloop-mcp-connect

skills.description: Position a Stellar Community Fund (SCF) idea against prior submissions before applying, using LumenLoop's SCF data. Use when the user is preparing an SCF application, wants to find similar past proposals, check what's been funded in an area, or sharpen their SCF positioning.
skills.name: scf-submission-radar

skills.description: Go from a Stellar product idea to a build path: pick the right Stellar primitives, check ecosystem prior art via LumenLoop, and route to the relevant build skill (Soroban, SDK/dapp, assets, oracles). Use when the user wants to start building something on Stellar and needs orientation on approach and existing work.
skills.name: stellar-builder-quickstart

skills.description: Audit and enrich a draft (blog post, talking points, tweet, announcement) against the LumenLoop Stellar ecosystem data — resolve the projects you mention to their canonical names and X/@ handles, pull supporting facts and citations from indexed content, and flag wrong handles or unsupported claims. Use when the user shares draft copy about Stellar and wants project handles/names/facts checked, or asks to fact-check, enrich, or add citations and links to a post.
skills.name: stellar-content-auditor

skills.description: Produce a dated, cited digest of recent Stellar ecosystem activity on a theme or named entity from LumenLoop's news, audio-video, event, and published research content. Use when the user asks what's new or recent or happening in an area of Stellar, or wants a roundup or newsletter-style summary.
skills.name: stellar-ecosystem-digest

skills.description: Map a sector or topic of the Stellar ecosystem into a landscape of projects, categories, regions, and related content using the LumenLoop directory and semantic search. Use when the user asks to survey, map, or get an overview of an area of Stellar (e.g. 'what RWA projects exist', 'map Stellar DeFi', 'who is building wallets').
skills.name: stellar-ecosystem-scout

skills.description: Find the right existing Stellar project or tool to integrate — wallet, oracle, anchor, RWA issuer, DEX, indexer — via the LumenLoop directory and semantic search, then route to the matching build skill. Use when the user asks 'what should I use for X on Stellar' or needs to pick an ecosystem dependency to integrate.
skills.name: stellar-integration-finder

skills.description: Build a due-diligence profile of a single Stellar project from the LumenLoop directory — details, related content, talks, SCF history, and similar projects. Use when the user names a project and asks to vet, profile, research, or summarize it, or wants a dossier or background on a Stellar team.
skills.name: stellar-project-dossier

tools.description: Find specific passages inside long AV transcripts via per-chunk embedding similarity. Use this when you want to quote what was said, not just "which video". Returns the parent video metadata plus the matched chunk text and its start offset.

query.description: Topic or phrase to match against transcript passages

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: find_av_passages

results.description: Array of AV items: AI summary, start_offset (opaque transcript chunk offset, not seconds), link.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: Array of AV items with AI summary, created_at (the recording's date — use it to judge and cite how recent the talk is), start_offset (an opaque transcript chunk offset for ordering passages within a recording — NOT playback seconds; do not render it as a timestamp or build a deep-link from it), and link. Transcript text itself is never returned — cite the link + the passage summary.
tools.when_to_use: Find WHERE in videos/podcasts/spaces a topic is discussed — returns AI summaries of the matching passages plus an opaque ordering offset (NOT a playback timestamp). Use when you need "the part of the talk where they discuss X".

tools.description: Find articles, AV content, events, and tweets that are semantically related to a project. Uses embedding similarity to find cross-domain content that DISCUSSES the project. Supports optional date/source/type filters for scoped research.

date_field.description: For articles only: force a specific date field. Default is COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at).

date_start.description: ISO date/timestamp lower bound (inclusive). For articles, applies to COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at) by default to avoid stale re-scrape pollution.

limit.description: Max results per content type (default 20). Effective rows = limit × number of types — keep it small and narrow `types` instead of raising it.

response_format.description: 'concise' omits long_summary from each row (smaller payloads); 'detailed' keeps it. Default: detailed.

sources.description: Filter by source. For articles: domain. For AV: channel. For events: platform. For tweets: user_screen_name.

types.description: Restrict to specific content types. Default: all 9 (knowledge = internal dated notes). Narrow this for smaller, faster results — fanning out across all types multiplies the row count.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: find_content_about_project

articles.description: Related articles with AI summaries + source links.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: Content grouped by type with AI summaries + source links + date, filtered to the project, recency-aware so recent coverage (incidents, launches) surfaces alongside canonical content. response_format 'concise' (external default) omits long_summary; pass 'detailed' to include it.
tools.when_to_use: All published content related to ONE project, keyed BY SLUG (resolve a name to a slug with search_directory first). For an open-ended topic use search_content_semantic; for entity-name mentions (incl. tokens/orgs) where you only have a name use find_content_by_entity.

tools.description: Find articles, AV, events, governance proposals, and SCF submissions that mention a specific named entity. Uses LLM-extracted entities (project/person/organization/token) from content_entities. Case-insensitive match on entity_name or exact match on normalized_slug. Use this when you want exact entity grounding, not semantic similarity.

content_type.description: Restrict to a specific content type

entity.description: Entity name (case-insensitive) or normalized slug. Examples: "Aquarius", "aquarius", "StellarOrg".

entity_type.description: Restrict to a specific entity type

min_confidence.description: Minimum LLM confidence to include (0.0-1.0). Default 0.5 filters out weak matches.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: find_content_by_entity

results.description: Array of content items with matched entity, confidence, metadata.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: Array of content items with the matched entity, confidence score, and content metadata.
tools.when_to_use: Find content mentioning a NAMED entity (project, organization, token) by case-insensitive name match with LLM-confidence filtering — works even when the entity has no directory slug. If you have a slug, prefer find_content_about_project.

tools.description: Find projects semantically similar to a given project using embedding cosine similarity. More accurate than tag/category matching.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: find_similar_projects_semantic

results.description: Array of similar projects: slug, title, description, similarity score.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: Array of projects ranked by cosine similarity: slug, title, description, similarity score.
tools.when_to_use: Find projects similar to a known project (by slug) via embedding similarity — better than tag/category matching. For keyword search use search_directory.

tools.description: Search SCF (Stellar Community Fund) submissions by embedding similarity. Pass a natural-language query for topic/category discovery or an existing submission slug to find similar prior proposals. Returns title, description, category, round, award_type, the linked_project_slug (the primary directory project, if any), and linked_project_slugs (the full set of directory projects this submission credits).

category.description: Restrict to a specific category (e.g. "Developer Tooling")

query.description: Natural-language query (e.g. "ZK proofs for compliance"). Mutually exclusive with `slug`.

round.description: Restrict to a specific SCF round (e.g. "SCF #29")

slug.description: Existing SCF submission slug (e.g. "recsAQHCghw9HIUvF") — returns semantically similar submissions. Mutually exclusive with `query`.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: find_similar_scf_submissions

results.description: Array of similar submissions with round/category/funding, linked_project_slug (primary credited project) and linked_project_slugs (full credited set).

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: Array of submissions ranked by similarity, with round/category/funding metadata, linked_project_slug (the primary credited directory project) and linked_project_slugs (the full set of credited directory projects).
tools.when_to_use: Explore SCF submissions by topic (natural-language query) or find proposals similar to an existing one (slug). Query and slug are mutually exclusive.

tools.description: Get the controlled vocabulary of categories. Use these exact values when setting project categories.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_categories

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: { count, categories: [...] } — each category: { id, name, slug }.
tools.when_to_use: Get the exact controlled vocabulary of project categories before filtering or labeling by category. Do not invent category strings.

tools.description: Get full details of a single document by ID from any collection. The av collection accepts either "videos" or "av" — they map to the same table.

collection.description: Content collection (videos and av both target the av table)

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_document

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: One document. External callers get the summary projection (title, url, summary, long_summary, tags, dates) — full body text and transcripts are not exposed; cite the returned url instead.
tools.when_to_use: Fetch one document by collection + numeric id, after a list/search tool gave you the id.

tools.description: Get details of a project from the ecosystem directory by slug. Pass compact=true to get a slim writer-friendly shape (~500 chars: slug, title, short description, category, tags, twitter_handles, website, other_names) — recommended for writer agents that just need to verify a slug and read @ handles. Default returns the full row (~8k chars including conflict tracking, source values, field policy).

compact.description: Return slim writer shape instead of full row. Cuts ~95% of payload size.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_project

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: One project object: slug, title, description, category, tags, links (website, x, github, …), based_in, operating_region, SCF awards. Internal-only fields (source values, conflicts, hidden links) are never included for external callers.
tools.when_to_use: Fetch one project BY SLUG. If you only have a name, resolve it to a slug with search_directory first — a guessed slug will 404. Pass compact=true when you only need identity fields for writing/citation (cuts ~95% of the payload).

tools.description: Get the controlled vocabulary of project tags. Use these exact tag values when setting directory project tags.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_project_tags_vocabulary

tags.description: project tag objects ({ id, name, slug })

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: { count, tags: [...] } — each tag: { id, name, slug }.
tools.when_to_use: Get the controlled tag vocabulary for DIRECTORY PROJECTS. For article/AV content tags use get_tags_vocabulary instead.

tools.description: Get all distinct region values (based_in + operating_region) currently used in the directory

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_regions

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.when_to_use: Get the distinct region values (based_in + operating_region) in use, before filtering projects geographically.

tools.description: Given a content item (article/av/event/research) return the directory projects it mentions, via the content_projects junction.

content_id.description: Numeric id of the content row (from get_document / search results).

content_type.description: Content type. Canonical singular: article | av | event | research | proposal (plural/alias accepted).

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_related_projects

results.description: Array of mentioned projects: slug, title, description, category.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: Array of mentioned projects with public info (slug, title, description, category).
tools.when_to_use: You have a CONTENT item (article/av/event/research id) and want the ecosystem projects it mentions — the "Mentioned projects" on lumenloop.com. The reverse direction (project → content) is find_content_about_project.

tools.description: Get Stellar Community Fund submissions linked to a project, including the full application/proposal text (markdown: sections, milestones, deliverables, budget detail), funding amount, award status, round, and category. Searches by slug or project name — pass at least one.

slug.description: Project slug (preferred — resolve names via search_directory first)

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_scf_submissions

submissions.description: SCF submissions: title, round, category, award_type, funding, full application/proposal text, links, linked_project_slug (primary credited project), linked_project_slugs (full credited set)

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: { count, submissions: [...] } — each: title, round, category, award_type, funding amounts, description, the full application/proposal text (markdown), links, linked_project_slug (the primary credited directory project) and linked_project_slugs (the full set of directory projects this submission credits).
tools.when_to_use: Get Stellar Community Fund submissions linked to a specific project. Pass slug (resolve a name via search_directory) or a fuzzy name. For topic-based exploration across all submissions use find_similar_scf_submissions.

tools.description: Get the controlled vocabulary of content tags from the orchestrator. These tags are used for articles and AV content, NOT for directory projects.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

tools.name: get_tags_vocabulary

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

tools.returns: Array of content tag objects (id, name).
tools.when_to_use: Get the controlled tag vocabulary for CONTENT (articles/AV). For directory project tags use get_project_tags_vocabulary instead.

tools.description: Browse/enumerate ONE collection by structured criteria — status, source, date, time period (upcoming/past events) — with sorting and pagination. Its optional "search" is the SAME exact case-insensitive SUBSTRING match on title and URL as search_documents (not body text, not meaning). For conceptual or question-phrased discovery, or when you do not know which collection holds the answer, use search_content_semantic instead. The av collection accepts either "videos" or "av".

collection.description: Content collection to query (videos and av both target the av table)

period.description: Events only: filter by time period

sort.description: Sort field. Articles: processed_at, publishing_date, created_at, domain, source. Events: start_at, processed_at, created_at. Videos: processed_at, publishing_date, created_at, source. Jobs: updated_at, last_seen_at, published_at, created_at.

invoke.auth: Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ key or OAuth token>

items.description: Document summaries: title, url, slug, summary, long_summary, tags, dates.

tools.description: List research drafts with optional filters. Used by drafter agents for dedup ("has anything similar been drafted lately?") and by agents needing to reference prior work. Returns preview fields only — call get_document(collection="research", id) for full body.

format.description: Filter by format ('sources' = partner source packs, 'structured' = partner schema-fills).

since.description: ISO date/timestamp; only rows with created_at >= this.

source.description: Filter by source prefix match (e.g. "agent:" for all agent-authored).

tools.when_to_use: Browse LumenLoop's own published research/editorial pieces (previews) — id, title, summary, links.

tools.description: Search all content semantically. Embeds the query text and finds similar articles, AV, events, and tweets via pgvector cosine distance. Use this (not search_documents/list_documents) whenever the query is conceptual or phrased as a question, or when you do not know which collection holds the answer; reserve search_documents for exact title/URL keyword lookup. Supports date/source/type filters — use these for scoped research queries like "find Stellar DeFi content from March" or "what did SDF post about privacy".

date_field.description: For articles only: force a specific date field. Default is COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at).

types.description: Restrict to specific content types. Default: all 9 (knowledge = internal dated notes). Narrow this for smaller, faster results — fanning out across all types multiplies the row count.

tools.when_to_use: Meaning-based search across ALL published content types at once (articles, AV, events, research) — the default discovery tool for "what has been said about X". For exact-term lookup in one collection use search_documents; for content about one specific project use find_content_about_project. For "latest/current" ecosystem facts (protocol version, program/fund status, recent incidents or launches) prefer this over live web — results are dated and the corpus updates continuously; cite the date. Use web only for facts the corpus cannot hold, e.g. upstream package/registry pins (crates.io, npm, GitHub release tags).
